name: Ballers
about: Ballers is a multi-sport social club that offers a premium indoor golf simulation experience alongside other athletic activities like pickleball, soccer, and tennis. The venue features multiple simulator bays designed for both casual play and serious practice, providing a professional-grade environment for golfers of all skill levels. Members and non-members have access to the facilities, with various membership tiers offering benefits such as discounted simulator time, priority booking, and complimentary guest passes. The club emphasizes a social, upscale atmosphere with amenities including a full-service restaurant, bar, and wellness facilities, making it a destination for both athletic activity and social gatherings.
why_golfers_like_it: Multiple professional-grade simulator bays for high-quality practice and play
Comprehensive club storage options and advanced booking windows for members
All-day access to simulator facilities with peak and off-peak availability
Integrated food and beverage options for a complete social golf experience
Access to global partner clubs, extending the membership benefits beyond the local venue

food_and_drink.notes: Full-service restaurant available on-site with a detailed food menu.
Full bar with cocktails available for service during and after play.

simulator_hardware.notes: Premium launch monitor technology for accurate ball and club tracking.

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, you will need to bring your own golf clubs to use the simulator bays.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: Outside food and drink are not permitted; the venue has its own full-service restaurant and bar available.

faq.question: How long does an 18-hole round take?
faq.answer: An 18-hole round typically takes between 1-2 hours depending on the number of players and pace of play.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, Ballers is kid-friendly and offers activities and programming suitable for families and children.
